reviewer.manual_testing_method: Manual keyboard, browser inspector, axe DevTools cross-check, zoom and text-spacing spot checks

findings.description: Search input uses placeholder text as its only visible prompt and has no programmatic label.
findings.expected_behavior: The search field has a persistent visible or programmatic label.
findings.actual_behavior: The field relies on placeholder text.

findings.description: Positive tabindex values require manual focus-order review before being treated as a barrier.
findings.component: skip links, navigation links, search controls, and floating widgets
findings.expected_behavior: Tab order follows the visible and logical page order.
findings.actual_behavior: Axe reports positive tabindex, but harmful ordering was not proven from the report alone.
findings.notes: Keep as review-only evidence unless A11yway demonstrates a harmful focus-order outcome.

findings.description: Nested menu or breadcrumb section text should not be reported as a label-in-name mismatch when the control name is a functional menu action such as Up one menu level.
findings.component: mobile navigation menu and breadcrumb controls
findings.expected_behavior: The functional menu control has an accessible name that explains the action.
findings.actual_behavior: Previous reports treated descendant section text as the visible label.
findings.notes: Suppress this pattern; still report true visible-label replacements such as Save draft named Close dialog.

findings.description: Sticky-header focus-obscured findings should require substantial coverage or repeat reproduction before high-priority outreach.
findings.component: focused controls near fixed or sticky page chrome
findings.expected_behavior: Focused controls remain visible and unobscured.
findings.actual_behavior: Prior report included partial hit-test coverage that was not clearly blocking.
findings.notes: Partial coverage below the tool threshold should not be reported.

findings.description: Document-wide horizontal overflow without clipped or overlapping content should be treated as review evidence, not a confirmed high-severity reflow failure.
findings.expected_behavior: At 400 percent zoom, content reflows without loss in one direction.
findings.actual_behavior: Small or document-level overflow did not by itself prove content loss.
findings.notes: Report high severity when overflow coincides with clipped content or overlapping interactive controls.
